subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships (e.g., After dinner that night we went looking for them).

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (e.g., agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (e.g., sitting, smiled, cries, happiness).

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use possessives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in addresses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk) verb tenses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., childhood).

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 2—3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur).

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the author of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.

subject: Math
description: Compose simple shapes to form larger shapes.

subject: Math
description: Model shapes in the world by building shapes from components (e.g., sticks and clay balls) and drawing shapes.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and compare two- and three-dimensional shapes, in different sizes and orientations, using informal language to describe their similarities, differences, parts (e.g., number of sides and vertices/"corners") and other attributes (e.g., having sides of equal length).

subject: Math
description: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes as two-dimensional (lying in a plane, "flat") or three-dimensional ("solid").

subject: Math
description: Correctly name shapes regardless of their orientations or overall size.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Describe measurable attributes of objects, such as length or weight. Describe several measurable attributes of a single object.

subject: Math
description: Describe and compare measurable attributes.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers from 11 to 19 into ten ones and some further ones, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each composition or decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 18 = 10 + 8); understand that these numbers are composed of ten ones and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.

subject: Math
description: Work with numbers 11—19 to gain foundations for place value.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 5.

subject: Math
description: For any number from 1 to 9, find the number that makes 10 when added to the given number, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record the answer with a drawing or equation.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 5 = 2 + 3 and 5 = 4 + 1).

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems, and add and subtract within 10, e.g., by using objects or drawings to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, fingers, mental images, drawings, sounds (e.g., claps), acting out situations, verbal explanations, expressions, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.

subject: Math
description: Compare two numbers between 1 and 10 presented as written numerals.

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group, e.g., by using matching and counting strategies.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?" questions about as many as 20 things arranged in a line, a rectangular array, or a circle, or as many as 10 things in a scattered configuration; given a number from 1—20, count out that many objects.

subject: Math
description: Understand that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one larger.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.

subject: Math
description: Know number names and the count sequence.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, thirds, half of, a third of, etc., and describe the whole as two halves, three thirds, four fourths. Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Explain why addition and subtraction strategies work, using place value and the properties of operations.

subject: Math
description: Mentally add 10 or 100 to a given number 100—900, and mentally subtract 10 or 100 from a given number 100—900.

subject: Math

Understand that in adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, one adds or subtracts hundreds and hundreds, tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose or decompose tens or hundreds.

subject: Math

description: Add and subtract within 20.

description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.

description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.

description: Look for and make use of structure.

description: Attend to precision.

description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or the same denominator by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.

subject: Math
description: Express whole numbers as fractions, and recognize fractions that are equivalent to whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and generate simple equivalent fractions, e.g., 1/2 = 2/4, 4/6 = 2/3). Explain why the fractions are equivalent, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.

subject: Math
description: Understand two fractions as equivalent (equal) if they are the same size, or the same point on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Explain equivalence of fractions in special cases, and compare fractions by reasoning about their size.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction a/b on a number line diagram by marking off a lengths 1/b from 0. Recognize that the resulting interval has size a/b and that its endpoint locates the number a/b on the number line.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction 1/b on a number line diagram by defining the interval from 0 to 1 as the whole and partitioning it into b equal parts. Recognize that each part has size 1/b and that the endpoint of the part based at 0 locates the number 1/b on the number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction as a number on the number line; represent fractions on a number line diagram.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction 1/b as the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts; understand a fraction a/b as the quantity formed by a parts of size 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of fractions as numbers.

subject: Math
description: Partition shapes into parts with equal areas. Express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.

subject: Math
description: Understand that shapes in different categories (e.g., rhombuses, rectangles, and others) may share attributes (e.g., having four sides), and that the shared attributes can define a larger category (e.g., quadrilaterals). Recognize rhombuses, rectangles, and squares as examples of quadrilaterals, and draw examples of quadrilaterals that do not belong to any of these subcategories.

subject: Math
description: Reason with shapes and their attributes.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world and mathematical problems involving perimeters of polygons, including finding the perimeter given the side lengths, finding an unknown side length, and exhibiting rectangles with the same perimeter and different areas or with the same area and different perimeters.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: recognize perimeter as an attribute of plane figures and distinguish between linear and area measures.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as additive. Find areas of rectilinear figures by decomposing them into non-overlapping rectangles and adding the areas of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.

subject: Math
description: Use tiling to show in a concrete case that the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths a and b + c is the sum of a × b and a × c. Use area models to represent the distributive property in mathematical reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Multiply side lengths to find areas of rectangles with whole-number side lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems, and represent whole-number products as rectangular areas in mathematical reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths by tiling it, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Relate area to the operations of multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Measure areas by counting unit squares (square cm, square m, square in, square ft, and improvised units).

subject: Math
description: A plane figure which can be covered without gaps or overlaps by n unit squares is said to have an area of n square units.

subject: Math
description: A square with side length 1 unit, called "a unit square," is said to have "one square unit" of area, and can be used to measure area.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as an attribute of plane figures and understand concepts of area measurement.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths using rulers marked with halves and fourths of an inch. Show the data by making a line plot, where the horizontal scale is marked off in appropriate units— whole numbers, halves, or quarters.

subject: Math
description: Draw a scaled picture graph and a scaled bar graph to represent a data set with several categories. Solve one- and two-step "how many more" and "how many less" problems using information presented in scaled bar graphs.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate liquid volumes and masses of objects using standard units of grams (g), kilograms (kg), and liters (l). Add, subtract, multiply, or divide to solve one-step word problems involving masses or volumes that are given in the same units, e.g., by using drawings (such as a beaker with a measurement scale) to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure time intervals in minutes. Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes, e.g., by representing the problem on a number line diagram.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and estimation of intervals of time, liquid volumes, and masses of objects.

subject: Math
description: Multiply one-digit whole numbers by multiples of 10 in the range 10—90 (e.g., 9 × 80, 5 × 60) using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 1000 using strategies and algorithms based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round whole numbers to the nearest 10 or 100.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.

subject: Math
description: Identify arithmetic patterns (including patterns in the addition table or multiplication table), and explain them using properties of operations.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step word problems using the four operations. Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity. Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies including rounding.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving the four operations, and identify and explain patterns in arithmetic.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply and divide within 100, using strategies such as the relationship between multiplication and division (e.g., knowing that 8 × 5 = 40, one knows 40 ÷ 5 = 8) or properties of operations. By the end of Grade 3, know from memory all products of two one-digit numbers.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide within 100.

description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as additive. Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V = l × w × h and V = b × h for rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths by packing it with unit cubes, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths, equivalently by multiplying the height by the area of the base. Represent threefold whole-number products as volumes, e.g., to represent the associative property of multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and addition and solve real world and mathematical problems involving volume.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft, and improvised units.

subject: Math
description: A solid figure which can be packed without gaps or overlaps using n unit cubes is said to have a volume of n cubic units.

subject: Math
description: A cube with side length 1 unit, called a "unit cube," is said to have "one cubic unit" of volume, and can be used to measure volume.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of solid figures and understand concepts of volume measurement.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Use operations on fractions for this grade to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Convert among different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system (e.g., convert 5 cm to 0.05 m), and use these conversions in solving multi-step, real world problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with multi-digit whole numbers and with decimals to hundredths.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round decimals to any place.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form, e.g., 347.392 = 3 × 100 + 4 × 10 + 7 × 1 + 3 × (1/10) + 9 × (1/100) + 2 × (1/1000).

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10, and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right and 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.

subject: Math
description: Understand the place value system.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules. Identify apparent relationships between corresponding terms. Form ordered pairs consisting of corresponding terms from the two patterns, and graph the ordered pairs on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns and relationships.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that record calculations with numbers, and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.

subject: Math
description: Use parentheses, brackets, or braces in numerical expressions, and evaluate expressions with these symbols.

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at school that are colorful).

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb to duck).

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (e.g., dog, dogs; wish, wishes).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly and express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions as desired to provide additional detail.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe familiar people, places, things, and events and, with prompting and support, provide additional detail.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions in order to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Confirm understanding of a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media by asking and answering questions about key details and requesting clarification if something is not understood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Continue a conversation through multiple exchanges.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others and taking turns speaking about the topics and texts under discussion).

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about kindergarten topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of books by a favorite author and express opinions about them).

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a single event or several loosely linked events, tell about the events in the order in which they occurred, and provide a reaction to what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces in which they tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book (e.g., My favorite book is…).

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent-reader texts with purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read common high-frequency words by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary or many of the most frequent sound for each consonant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).

description: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.

description: Name the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each in presenting the ideas or information in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear (e.g., what moment in a story an illustration depicts).

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, name the author and illustrator of a story and define the role of each in telling the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of texts (e.g., storybooks, poems).

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, and major events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to hundredths by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two decimals refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual model.

subject: Math
description: Use decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.

subject: Math
description: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n × a)/(n × b) by using visual fraction models, with attention to how the number and size of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.

Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real world and mathematical problems, e.g., by using an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.

subject: Math
description: An angle that turns through n one-degree angles is said to have an angle measure of n degrees.

subject: Math
description: An angle is measured with reference to a circle with its center at the common endpoint of the rays, by considering the fraction of the circular arc between the points where the two rays intersect the circle. An angle that turns through 1/360 of a circle is called a "one-degree angle," and can be used to measure angles.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint, and understand concepts of angle measurement:

subject: Math

description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.

description: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule. Identify apparent features of the pattern that were not explicit in the rule itself.

subject: Math
description: Generate and analyze patterns.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Multiply or divide to solve word problems involving multiplicative comparison, e.g., by using drawings and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem, distinguishing multiplicative comparison from additive comparison.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.

description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.

description: Use appropriate tools strategically.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.

description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

subject: Science

subject: Science
description: Earth's Place in the Universe

subject: Math
description: Describe qualitatively the functional relationship between two quantities by analyzing a graph (e.g., where the function is increasing or decreasing, linear or nonlinear). Sketch a graph that exhibits the qualitative features of a function that has been described verbally.

subject: Math
description: Construct a function to model a linear relationship between two quantities. Determine the rate of change and initial value of the function from a description of a relationship or from two (x, y) values, including reading these from a table or from a graph. Interpret the rate of change and initial value of a linear function in terms of the situation it models, and in terms of its graph or a table of values.

subject: Math
description: Use functions to model relationships between quantities.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the equation y = mx + b as defining a linear function, whose graph is a straight line; give examples of functions that are not linear.

subject: Math
description: Compare properties of two functions each represented in a different way (algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, or by verbal descriptions).

subject: Math
description: Understand that a function is a rule that assigns to each input exactly one output. The graph of a function is the set of ordered pairs consisting of an input and the corresponding output.

subject: Math
description: Define, evaluate, and compare functions.

subject: Math
description: Understand that patterns of association can also be seen in bivariate categorical data by displaying frequencies and relative frequencies in a two-way table. Construct and interpret a two-way table summarizing data on two categorical variables collected from the same subjects. Use relative frequencies calculated for rows or columns to describe possible association between the two variables.

subject: Math
description: Use the equation of a linear model to solve problems in the context of bivariate measurement data, interpreting the slope and intercept.

subject: Math
description: Know that straight lines are widely used to model relationships between two quantitative variables. For scatter plots that suggest a linear association, informally fit a straight line, and informally assess the model fit by judging the closeness of the data points to the line.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association between two quantities. Describe patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative association, linear association, and nonlinear association.

subject: Math
description: Investigate patterns of association in bivariate data.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems leading to two linear equations in two variables.

subject: Math

description: Understand that solutions to a system of two linear equations in two variables correspond to points of intersection of their graphs, because points of intersection satisfy both equations simultaneously.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve pairs of simultaneous linear equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations with rational number coefficients, including equations whose solutions require expanding expressions using the distributive property and collecting like terms.

subject: Math

description: Look for and make use of structure.

description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

description: Analyze data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts

description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.

description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.

description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.

description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.

description: Relating the choice of measures of center and variability to the shape of the data distribution and the context in which the data were gathered.

description: Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity, thought of as the dependent variable, in terms of the other quantity, thought of as the independent variable. description: Write an inequality of the form x > c or x < c to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem. Recognize that inequalities of the form x > c or x < c have infinitely many solutions; represent solutions of such inequalities on number line diagrams.

description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by writing and solving equations of the form x + p = q and px = q for cases in which p, q and x are all nonnegative rational numbers.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math

description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, surface area, and volume.
